dimensions.name: testability
dimensions.evidence: Most acceptance criteria are mechanically checkable with explicit files, exit codes, path rules, schema contracts, and timing/idempotency expectations.

dimensions.name: completeness
dimensions.evidence: The provided specs do not fully refine several listed deliverables/ACs, notably `source_todos` runtime wiring and the full `frw upgrade` migration behavior for legacy config/specialists installs.

dimensions.name: consistency
dimensions.evidence: The config spec names `gate_policy_default`, while the acceptance criteria call for a global `gate_policy` default, indicating a contract mismatch.

dimensions.name: test-scenario-coverage
dimensions.evidence: The specs name test files and broad coverage areas, but they do not provide explicit WHEN/THEN scenarios with concrete verification commands for each non-trivial criterion.

dimensions.name: implementability
dimensions.evidence: A developer can start on many parts, but missing edge-case behavior for migration/back-compat paths means common implementation questions remain unresolved.
